content: The linguistic definition of 'reflexion' as both 'physical reflection of light' and 'deliberate cognitive reflection' can be mapped to LLM self-reflection mechanisms to frame a new multi-perspective reflection framework: just as a physical mirror reflects light along a consistent geometric angle, different inconsistent solving perspectives in LLM self-reflection act like textured, off-angle reflectors that bounce problem reasoning off unconsidered cognitive surfaces to reveal hidden flaws. This re-framing gives a concrete, intuitive metaphor to guide the design of more robust LLM self-reflection systems.
